name: Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix yeast, water, and 1 tablespoon sugar in a bowl. Let the mixture rise.
2. In a pot, combine 1 quart milk with 1/2 cup sugar and 1/2 pound butter. Scald over medium heat until it reaches just below boiling point; then remove from heat to cool slightly.
3. In another bowl, beat eggs and mix in the remaining butter and sugar until well combined.
4. Gradually add flour to the egg mixture, mixing well. Stir in vanilla extract, the egg mixture, milk mixture, yeast (or 1/4 pound), and salt.
5. Knead the dough until it is smooth and no longer sticky; the more it is mixed, the better the bread will turn out.
6. Place the dough in a lightly greased bowl and cover with a towel. Let it rise until doubled in size.
7. Punch down the dough to release air bubbles, then shape it into loaves and place them in prepared pans. Allow to rise again for about an hour.
8. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Bake for 30 minutes at this temperature. Reduce heat to 350°F (175°C) and continue baking for another 30-40 minutes, or until golden brown and cooked through.
From The Cookie Lady's Files
Posted by COOKIE.LADY [Cookie] on Mar 16, 1993
